briefdescription.para: Data vector to be deconvolved by concrete scalar methods.
Source-wavelet estimate used by concrete scalar methods.
Deconvolved output vector produced by process.
Output shaping wavelet applied by scalar deconvolution methods.
Construct an empty scalar deconvolution operator.
Construct from Metadata.

detaileddescription.para: Initializes the shaping wavelet from md and reserves the data, wavelet, and result vectors using the operator_nfft value in md.

briefdescription.para: Construct with loaded data and source-wavelet vectors.

parameterdescription.para: source-wavelet estimate used by concrete deconvolution methods.

briefdescription.para: Copy constructor preserving loaded vectors and shaping wavelet.
Load all data required for decon.

detaileddescription.para: This method loads both the data vector and wavelet estimates as simple std::vectors. Timing must be maintained externally. This or the pair of methods loaddata and loadwavelet must be called before process. This method has a slight advantage in efficiency over successive calls to loaddata and loadwavelet for several reasons, but the difference is small.

briefdescription.para: Run the concrete scalar deconvolution algorithm.

detaileddescription.para: Derived classes implement this method and store their deconvolved output in the result vector.

briefdescription.para: Assign loaded wavelet, data, and result vectors from parent.
Return the current deconvolution result vector.

detaileddescription.para: This method does nothing, but needs to be defined to avoid gcc compile errors in programs using children of this class.
Change the shaping wavelet that will be applied to output.

The suite of algorithms here use the concept of a shaping wavelet thoughout. The shaping wavelet for most applications should have a zero phase impulse response. This method changes the wavelet set with the operator.
getter for ShapingWavelet stored with the operator.

briefdescription.para: Return the output shaping wavelet.

detaileddescription.para: Wang and Pavlis (2016) call this wavelet ws(t). GID methods convolve the sparse impulse response with this wavelet to form the finite-duration receiver-function representation used for stacking and imaging.

briefdescription.para: Legacy alias for output_shaping_wavelet.

detaileddescription.para: Older MsPASS code called the output shaping wavelet "ideal_output". New code and documentation should prefer output_shaping_wavelet, which matches the terminology of Wang and Pavlis (2016).

briefdescription.para: Return the actual output of the deconvolution operator.

detaileddescription.para: The actual output is defined as w^-1*w and is compable to resolution kernels in linear inverse theory. Although not required we would normally expect this function to be peaked at 0. Offsets from 0 would imply a bias.

briefdescription.para: Alias for actual_output using inverse-theory terminology.
Return a FIR represention of the inverse filter.

detaileddescription.para: After any deconvolution is computed one can sometimes produce a finite impulse response (FIR) respresentation of the inverse filter.

briefdescription.para: Return a FIR inverse filter with a specified parent time origin.

parameterdescription.para: time origin assigned to the returned CoreTimeSeries.

briefdescription.para: Return appropriate quality measures.

detaileddescription.para: Each operator commonly has different was to measure the quality of the result. This method should return these in a generic Metadata object.

briefdescription.para: Build QC Metadata fields common to scalar deconvolution operators.

detaileddescription.para: The returned Metadata records the operator name, whether processing has run, whether input vectors are loaded, the input and output vector sizes, and the shaping-wavelet type, sample interval, and FFT length.

briefdescription.para: Base class decon operator for single station 3C decon (receiver functions).

detaileddescription.para: A class of algorithms exist for computing so called receiver functions. Simple for this application means a method that is applied to a single station's data and computed in a scalar time series sense. Thus the interface assumes we want to always at least load an estimate of the source wavelet for deconvolution and the data to which it is to be applied.

The design of this interface was made more complicated by a design goal to allow application of different conventional methods as the first step in the generalized iterative method. The generalized method can select one of the children of this base class.
